sourceMovie.title: Run All Night
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Run All Night (2015) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Jaume Collet-Serra's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Run All Night clicked for you, War (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Hot Fuzz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Run All Night clicked for you, Hot Fuzz (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Run All Night?
faq.answer: Try War, Copshop, Hot Fuzz—each shares DNA with Run All Night while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Run All Night?
faq.answer: Run All Night (2015) is best described as Action, Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Run All Night part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Run All Night Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
